question:
Suppose we have selected a random sample of $n=36$ observations from a population with mean equal to 80 and standard deviation equal to 6 . It is known that the population is not extremely skewed.
a. Sketch the relative frequency distributions for the population and for the sampling distribution of the sample mean, $ ar{x}$.
b. Find the probability that $ ar{x}$ will be larger than 82 .
answer:
a. We do not know the exact shape of the population relative frequency distribution, but we do know that it should be centered about $\mu=80$, its spread should be measured by $\sigma=6$, and it is not highly skewed. One possibility is shown in Figure 5.11a. From the Central Limit Theorem, we know that the sampling distribution of $ ar{x}$ will be approximately normal because the sampled population distribution is not extremely skewed. We also know that the sampling distribution will have mean and standard deviation

$$
\mu_{ ar{x}}=\mu=80 \quad 	ext { and } \quad \sigma_{ ar{x}}= rac{\sigma}{\sqrt{n}}= rac{6}{\sqrt{36}}=1
$$


The sampling distribution of $ ar{x}$ is shown in Figure 5.11b.
b. The probability that $ ar{x}$ will exceed 82 is equal to the darker shaded area in Figure 5.12. To find this area, we need to find the $z$-value corresponding to $ ar{x}=82$. Recall that the standard normal random variable $z$ is the difference between any normally distributed random variable and its mean, expressed in units of its standard deviation. Because $ ar{x}$ is approximately a normally distributed random variable with mean $\mu_{ ar{x}}=\mu$ and $\sigma_{ ar{x}}=\sigma / \sqrt{ } n$, it follows that the standard normal $z$-value corresponding to the sample mean, $ ar{x}$, is

$$
z= rac{(	ext { Normal random variable })-(	ext { Mean })}{	ext { Standard deviation }}= rac{ ar{x}-\mu_{ ar{x}}}{\sigma_{ ar{x}}}
$$


Therefore, for $ ar{x}=82$, we have

$$
z= rac{ ar{x}-\mu_{ ar{x}}}{\sigma_{ ar{x}}}= rac{82-80}{1}=2
$$
The area $A$ in Figure 5.12 corresponding to $z=2$ is given in the table of areas under the normal curve (see Table II in Appendix D: Tables) as .4772 . Therefore, the tail area corresponding to the probability that $ ar{x}$ exceeds 82 is

$$
P( ar{x}>82)=P(z>2)=.5-.4772=.0228
$$

meta_info.caption: Using the Central Limit Theorem to Find a Probability
meta_info.section: 5.3 The Sampling Distribution of the Sample Mean and the Central Limit Theorem
